Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Eliot Teitelbaum, Esq. (310.440.5276; eliot@ktcounsel.com) of Koorenny & Teitelbaum, LLP for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA UNITED MECHANICAL, INC.'S Motion To Enforce Settlement.

Plaintiff and cross-defendant California United Mechanical, Inc.'s motion to enforce settlement agreement is denied. Per the text of CCP 664.6 and case law, the summary procedure of section 664.6 is available only when there is "pending litigation" at the time the parties entered into the settlement agreement that is sought to be enforced. (Housing Group v. United National Insurance Co. (2001) 90 Cal. App. 4th 1106). Any effort by the parties to invoke section 664.6 absent pending litigation at the time of the settlement agreement that is sought to be enforced is unavailing. (Id.). Because it is undisputed that there was no pending litigation when the United/Equinox settlement agreement was entered into, the motion must be denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)
